Operation 'Upsert' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original summary 'Upsert' is too vague and not action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about inputs (batch format, metadata support), outputs (return value), namespace context, and the complete scope of what gets overwritten. Enhanced version clarifies the operation accepts batches, explains the overwrite behavior comprehensively, specifies return value, and emphasizes namespace isolation for better AI agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: The Upsert operation writes vectors into a namespace. If a new value is upserted for an existing vector id, it will overwrite the previous value.
data.summary_suggestion: Write or update vectors in a namespace
data.description_suggestion: Writes vectors into a specified namespace, creating new vector entries or updating existing ones. If a vector with the same ID already exists, its dense data, sparse data, and metadata will be overwritten with the provided values. Accepts a batch of vectors with IDs, dense/sparse data, and optional metadata. Returns the count of successfully upserted vectors. Operates within a single namespace context.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Upsert' is too vague and not action-oriented. Description lacks critical details about inputs (batch format, metadata support), outputs (return value), namespace context, and the complete scope of what gets overwritten. Enhanced version clarifies the operation accepts batches, explains the overwrite behavior comprehensively, specifies return value, and emphasizes namespace isolation for better AI agent decision-making about when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'create_collection' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too minimal. Added details about required inputs (CollectionDefinition), what the operation returns, and side effects (collection availability). This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: This operation creates a Pinecone collection.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Pinecone collection from a snapshot of an index. Requires a CollectionDefinition specifying the source index name and collection name. Returns the created collection object with metadata including creation timestamp and status. The collection becomes available for querying after creation.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added details about required inputs (CollectionDefinition), what the operation returns, and side effects (collection availability). This helps AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.current_description: This operation deletes an existing collection.

message: Operation 'create_index' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description explains configurable parameters well but lacks clarity on what is returned and side effects. Enhanced description to specify return value and index readiness state, helping AI agents understand the complete operation lifecycle.

data.current_description: This operation creates a Pinecone index. You can use it to specify the measure of similarity, the dimension of vectors to be stored in the index, the numbers of replicas to use, and more.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new Pinecone index with specified configuration. Accepts an IndexDefinition specifying similarity metric (e.g., cosine, euclidean, dotproduct), vector dimensionality, pod type, number of replicas, and other parameters. Returns the created index object with metadata. The index becomes ready for vector operations after initialization.
data.reasoning: Original description explains configurable parameters well but lacks clarity on what is returned and side effects. Enhanced description to specify return value and index readiness state, helping AI agents understand the complete operation lifecycle.

message: Operation 'delete_index' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_side_effects. The current description is too minimal and lacks critical information about the destructive nature of this operation. AI agents need to understand that this is a permanent action affecting all data within the index, and that it requires authentication. Added context about side effects and irreversibility.

data.current_description: This operation deletes an existing index.
data.description_suggestion: Deletes an existing index and all associated data. This operation permanently removes the index specified by indexName, including all vectors, metadata, and namespaces within it. This action cannot be undone. Requires appropriate API key authentication.
data.reasoning: The current description is too minimal and lacks critical information about the destructive nature of this operation. AI agents need to understand that this is a permanent action affecting all data within the index, and that it requires authentication. Added context about side effects and irreversibility.

Added details about the request schema requirement, return value, and that configuration changes may have latency. Improved summary to be more specific about what is being configured.

data.current_description: This operation specifies the pod type and number of replicas for an index.
data.summary_suggestion: Configure index pod type and replicas
data.description_suggestion: Modifies the configuration of an existing index by updating its pod type and replica count. This operation allows scaling the index infrastructure to meet performance and availability requirements. The request body should contain the desired IndexConfiguration. Returns the updated index configuration. Changes may take time to propagate.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about what inputs are expected and what is returned. Added details about the request schema requirement, return value, and that configuration changes may have latency. Improved summary to be more specific about what is being configured.

message: Operation 'Query' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks details about input parameters (filters, result limits) and doesn't mention that metadata can be returned. Enhanced to clarify that both dense and sparse vectors are supported, filters are optional, and results are ranked by the index's similarity metric.

data.current_description: The `Query` operation searches a namespace, using a query vector. It retrieves the ids of the most similar items in a namespace, along with their similarity scores.
data.summary_suggestion: Search vectors by similarity in namespace
data.description_suggestion: Searches a namespace using a query vector to find the most similar vectors. The operation accepts a query vector (dense and/or sparse), optional metadata filters, and a limit on the number of results. Returns the IDs of the most similar vectors along with their similarity scores and optional metadata. Results are ranked by similarity according to the index's configured metric.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks details about input parameters (filters, result limits) and doesn't mention that metadata can be returned. Enhanced to clarify that both dense and sparse vectors are supported, filters are optional, and results are ranked by the index's similarity metric.

message: Operation 'Delete' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details. Original summary is too vague. Description is repetitive and lacks details about input parameters. Enhanced to clarify that multiple vectors can be deleted, that optional filters are supported, and what the operation returns. Improved summary to be more specific and action-oriented.

data.current_description: The `Delete` operation deletes vectors, by id, from a single namespace. You can delete items by their id, from a single namespace.
data.summary_suggestion: Delete vectors by ID from namespace
data.description_suggestion: Deletes one or more vectors from a specified namespace by their vector IDs. The operation accepts a list of vector IDs to delete and an optional metadata filter to further restrict which vectors are deleted. Only vectors matching both the ID list and filter criteria (if provided) will be removed. Returns confirmation of the deletion operation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too vague. Description is repetitive and lacks details about input parameters. Enhanced to clarify that multiple vectors can be deleted, that optional filters are supported, and what the operation returns. Improved summary to be more specific and action-oriented.

message: Operation 'Fetch' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about input parameters (list of IDs) and doesn't explain behavior for missing vectors. Enhanced to clarify that multiple vectors can be fetched, what happens when IDs don't exist, and provided use case context for AI agents.

data.current_description: The `Fetch` operation looks up and returns vectors, by ID, from a single namespace. The returned vectors include the vector data and/or metadata.
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve vectors by ID from namespace
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves one or more vectors from a specified namespace by their vector IDs. The operation accepts a list of vector IDs and returns the corresponding vectors with their dense vector data and associated metadata. If a vector ID does not exist, it is omitted from the results. Useful for retrieving specific vectors after a query or for data validation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks clarity about input parameters (list of IDs) and doesn't explain behavior for missing vectors. Enhanced to clarify that multiple vectors can be fetched, what happens when IDs don't exist, and provided use case context for AI agents.

message: Operation 'Update' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, inconsistent_terminology, missing_input_details. Original summary incorrectly says 'Fetch' instead of describing an update operation. Description uses inconsistent terminology ('vector' vs 'vectors') and is unclear about partial vs full updates. Enhanced to clarify that updates are partial (unspecified fields are preserved), that both vector data and metadata can be updated independently, and that vectors must exist.

data.current_description: The `Update` operation updates vector in a namespace. If a value is included, it will overwrite the previous value. If a set_metadata is included, the values of the fields specified in it will be added or overwrite the previous value.
data.description_suggestion: Updates one or more vectors in a specified namespace by modifying their vector data and/or metadata. The operation accepts a vector ID, optional new vector data, and optional metadata updates. If vector data is provided, it replaces the existing vector. If metadata is provided, specified fields are added or overwritten while preserving unspecified fields. Vectors must exist in the namespace to be updated.
data.reasoning: Original summary incorrectly says 'Fetch' instead of describing an update operation. Description uses inconsistent terminology ('vector' vs 'vectors') and is unclear about partial vs full updates. Enhanced to clarify that updates are partial (unspecified fields are preserved), that both vector data and metadata can be updated independently, and that vectors must exist.
